attacking_style: Launches decisive solo attacks with 12km remaining on final climb sequences, timing moves after aggressive pace-making has thinned the field. Prefers late-race ambushes over early isolation.
team_tactics: Relies on aggressive pace-making from others to set up attacks rather than dedicated team support. Positions conservatively in breakaways, prioritizing cooperation until final climb.
terrain_preference: Multi-climb finales with 10-15km technical descents or rolling terrain to finish. Thrives when repeated climbs create selection before final push. Struggles on sustained steep final climbs at high altitude.
vulnerability: Age-related decline evident by 2019 (35 years old) with struggles on sustained final climbs. Dropped when pace remains high without tactical variation opportunities.
key_patterns: Attacks with precisely 12km to go on final climbs across multiple wins (2 observations)
Waits for others to force pace before capitalizing, not initiating selection himself (3 observations)
Cooperates in breakaways until final climb rather than testing companions early (2 observations)
Performs best in multi-climb finales versus single sustained efforts (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: Despite 2019 Tour de Suisse wins, age showing with decline on sustained climbs at Paris-Nice. Peak tactical ability but declining physical capacity at 35.
